channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lobby Group Sues Speakers Wetang'ula and Kingi for Campaigning During Tenure
item.description: A lobby group has filed a petition at the High Court seeking to bar National Assembly speaker Moses Wetang'ula and Senate speaker Amason Kingi from participating in partisan political campaigns while ...

item.title: Farage triggers Clacton by-election amid political uncertainty
item.description: Nigel Farage has initiated a by-election in his Essex constituency, Clacton, despite opposition from major parties. The move comes as Count Binface, a satirical candidate representing the Monster Ravi...

item.title: India's Government Announces Major Policy Reforms for Sustainable Development

item.title: Ministers Oversee Enhancements for Airport Passenger Experience

item.title: Balochistan Attacks Result in 42 Martyrs and 54 Terrorists Neutralized
item.description: The DG ISPR reported three major terrorist incidents in Balochistan over four days, resulting in the martyrdom of 42 security personnel and neutralization of 54 terrorists. The first attack on July 4/...

item.title: Edwin Sifuna's Rising Popularity as a Potential Ruto Challenger in Kenya's 2027 Election
item.description: The article discusses the growing political influence of Edwin Sifuna, a Nairobi senator, within Kenya's opposition. Sifuna's support among Gen Z voters is seen as a potential threat to President Will...

item.title: Adesuwa Okunbo-Rhodes' journey from investment banking to founding Aruwa Capital and addressing Nigeria's SME funding gap
item.description: Adesuwa Okunbo-Rhodes, who started her global banking career at 18, now drives impactful investments as head of Aruwa Capital. She began her finance career at Lehman Brothers, later moving to J.P. Mor...

item.title: Arthur Fery's Wimbledon Triumph: A British Hopeful's Historic Run
item.description: Arthur Fery, a 23-year-old British tennis player ranked 114th in the world, has defied expectations by reaching the Wimbledon singles quarter-finals as a wildcard entry. His unexpected victory over fo...

item.title: Kerala Debris Slip: Search Continues for Five Missing After Tunnel Construction Site Collapse
item.description: A deadly debris slip occurred at a tunnel road construction site in Wayanad district of Kerala, India, on July 7, 2026, resulting in three fatalities and leaving five people missing. Rescue operations...

item.title: Constitutional Court's NHI Ruling and Its Impact on South Africa's Health Policy
item.description: The article discusses the significance of the Constitutional Court's ruling on South Africa's National Health Insurance (NHI) legislation, emphasizing its role in ensuring proper legislative processes...

item.title: University of Nairobi Graduate Struggles with Job Hunt After 11 Years

item.title: China Considers Restricting Overseas Access to Advanced AI Models to Protect National Security

item.title: Expedition Uncovers Sunken Ship Quest, Final Vessel of Ernest Shackleton

item.title: Will Smith and Jada Pinkett Reunite After Years of Secret Separation
item.description: Will Smith and Jada Pinkett Smith have reportedly resumed living together after nearly seven years of secret separation. The couple quietly split in 2016 but kept their relationship private until 2023...

item.title: AmaZulu King Appoints New Spokesperson, Ending Thulani Zulu's Era

item.title: Ghana and South Africa Postpone Bilateral Talks Amid Anti-Migrant Tensions
item.description: Ghana has postponed planned bilateral meetings with South Africa scheduled for August, citing rising anti-migrant violence in the country. Government spokesperson Felix Kwakye Ofosu stated the violenc...
